---
title: "Electrolyser Power Electronics"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

Electrolyser power electronics condition electrical power before it enters the PEM stack. The need for these components differs between centralised and decentralised hydrogen.

## Cost Model

| Item | Assumption |
|---|---:|
| Power electronics CAPEX | 87 EUR/kW |
| Scaling factor | 1.00 |

Power electronics are treated as mature, modular components and do not receive central-facility economies of scale.

## Energy Balance

For centralised electrolysis, the infield AC power is stepped down and rectified to DC before entering the stack. Current explicit losses are:

| Component | Loss |
|---|---:|
| Rectifier | 1.0% |
| Transformer | 0.5% |

For decentralised electrolysis, these platform-side components are absent because the stack connects to the turbine DC link.

## Availability

Availability is currently included in the electrolyser availability factor unless separated in a later version.

## Architecture Applicability

| Architecture | Treatment |
|---|---|
| Centralised hydrogen | Included |
| Decentralised hydrogen | Excluded or strongly reduced due to DC-link integration |
| Electricity export | Covered by turbine and HVDC electrical infrastructure pages |
